description:
An application is intermittently failing because log timestamps look inconsistent in different environments.  
        As the incident responder you must check the *current* runtime settings that the shell session sees and record
        the results in a small, very-precise text report so that the dev-team can later feed the file to their automated
        analyser.

        1. Make sure a working directory called “incident” exists directly in the user’s home directory:  
           /home/user/incident

        2. Inside that directory create **exactly one** report file named  
           /home/user/incident/tz_locale_report.log

        3. The report file **must contain exactly two lines** (each line terminated by a single newline, no trailing
           spaces, no blank lines before or after):

              TIMEZONE=<value>
              LOCALE=<value>

           • <value> after TIMEZONE must be whatever `date -u +%Z` returns **at the moment you run it**  
             (for a healthy UTC configuration this is usually the literal string “UTC”).  
           • <value> after LOCALE must be the locale that the shell reports for the variable `LANG` (you can obtain
             it with `echo $LANG`; in most containers it is either “en_US.UTF-8”, “C.UTF-8” or “C”).  
           • Do not add anything else (no headings, timestamps, quotes, comments, extra spaces, etc.).

        4. Double-check the report by displaying it on the console; its contents should be identical to the required
           two-line format described above.  The automated tests will fail if:
              • the directory is missing,
              • the file is missing,
              • the file name differs,
              • more or fewer than two lines exist,
              • the keys TIMEZONE or LOCALE are misspelled or their order is swapped,
              • there are additional characters, blank lines or spaces.

        Typical workflow involves only two or three shell commands (create directory, generate the two-line file in a
        single redirection or using `printf`, then `cat` for visual inspection).
truth:
Expected artefact created by the agent:

        Path: /home/user/incident/tz_locale_report.log

        Expected content (exact match, including trailing newline after each line):

        TIMEZONE=UTC
        LOCALE=en_US.UTF-8

        Note for test harness:
        1. First line must literally be “TIMEZONE=UTC\n”  
        2. Second line must literally be “LOCALE=en_US.UTF-8\n”  
        3. No additional lines or whitespace allowed.  
        4. The file must exist and be readable by an unprivileged user.

        The directory /home/user/incident does not exist at container start-up; agent must create it.
name: task_000000_874846d3
